trending_topics.context: 2 posts, 3.17x baseline rate — mentioning Terry Watanabe

sample_posts.text:
Terry Watanabe on Gambling Away $350 Million Fortune
• Sold his company (inherited from father) for $400M, netting $350M personally
• Compulsive gambler, ~$127M lost in about a year
• Annual wagering escalated from $4.2M (2005) to $81M (2006) to $825M (2007)
• Currently Terry is broke, living on Social Security
• The core irony he acknowledges: at 5% interest, that fortune would passively throw off ~$17.5M/year forever. His own cited "turning point" was online blackjack in his 30s, where he quietly lost ~$30M before Vegas ever entered the picture
(good wake up call for all the gamblers here, i

The Economics of a Neocloud
Intense market shortage has created highly lucrative economics and incredibly short payback periods for data center investments
https://degentradingdaily.substack.com/p/the-economics-of-a-neocloud-5-jul

tweet.text:
A conversation with @JonathanRoss321, founder of @GroqInc. This is the first podcast that Jonathan talks about his $20 billion partnership with NVIDIA. 

In this conversation, we talk about his decade building Groq: why West Coast VCs kept passing on the company, how fast Jensen works, overcoming near-death experiences, and why he thinks the entire AI age turns on a single skill — knowing what to ask.

Hope you enjoy this conversation:

0:00 The $20 Billion NVIDIA Deal Closed In 3 Weeks
0:25 Why GPUs And LPUs Are Better Together
1:46 When AI Talks To AI, Speed Wins
3:30 Always Start With A Hob

trending_topics.context: 3 posts, 4.09x baseline rate — mentioning Optimism, SpaceX, NVIDIA, OpenAI

tweet.text:
The GenAI economy has generated $110 billion in sales over the past 12 months. It is growing fast. On an annualized basis, the revenue run rate exceeds $175 billion.

These numbers took us several months to construct, and as far as we know, it’s the first bottom-up, deduplicated measure of consumer and enterprise AI spending across the full stack.

We are releasing this research today in our first The State of the AI Economy report.

https://intelligence.exponentialview.co/

Partners from two top US venture firms, Benchmark and Founders Fund, say they are uncomfortable with the current funding frenzy because it reminds them of 2021.

“I’m very uncomfortable.....because it feels like we're getting back to a point where prices have become untethered from reality. It reminds me a lot of 2021,” says Trae Stephens of Founders Fund.

Everett Randle of Benchmark adds: “The similarity I see with 2021 is that back then there was an almost unanimous sense of optimism because investors were making a tremendous amount of money.”

“I think this duality is what defines a bubble

How do you get value out of commodities?

If everyone has access to the same open weight models, where does the value come from?

Value creation comes from the effective application of commodities.

Fuel is a commodity. But two companies with access to the same fuel can produce radically different outcomes. One uses it inefficiently. The other uses it to power a fleet that is tightly coordinated around demand, supply, inventory, routing, labor, timing, and real world exception handling.

The difference is not the fuel.

The difference is the operational system that turns fuel into movement, mo

A conversation with @JonathanRoss321, founder of @GroqInc. This is the first podcast that Jonathan talks about his $20 billion partnership with NVIDIA. 

In this conversation, we talk about his decade building Groq: why West Coast VCs kept passing on the company, how fast Jensen works, overcoming near-death experiences, and why he thinks the entire AI age turns on a single skill — knowing what to ask.

Hope you enjoy this conversation:

0:25 Why GPUs And LPUs Are Better Together
1:46 When AI Talks To AI, Speed Wins
3:30 Always Start With A Hob

tweet.text:
The GenAI economy has generated $110 billion in sales over the past 12 months. It is growing fast. On an annualized basis, the revenue run rate exceeds $175 billion.

These numbers took us several months to construct, and as far as we know, it’s the first bottom-up, deduplicated measure of consumer and enterprise AI spending across the full stack.

We are releasing this research today in our first The State of the AI Economy report.

Partners from two top US venture firms, Benchmark and Founders Fund, say they are uncomfortable with the current funding frenzy because it reminds them of 2021.

“I’m very uncomfortable.....because it feels like we're getting back to a point where prices have become untethered from reality. It reminds me a lot of 2021,” says Trae Stephens of Founders Fund.

Everett Randle of Benchmark adds: “The similarity I see with 2021 is that back then there was an almost unanimous sense of optimism because investors were making a tremendous amount of money.”

“I think this duality is what defines a bubble

sample_posts.text:
Terry Watanabe on Gambling Away $350 Million Fortune
• Sold his company (inherited from father) for $400M, netting $350M personally
• Compulsive gambler, ~$127M lost in about a year
• Annual wagering escalated from $4.2M (2005) to $81M (2006) to $825M (2007)
• Currently Terry is broke, living on Social Security
• The core irony he acknowledges: at 5% interest, that fortune would passively throw off ~$17.5M/year forever. His own cited "turning point" was online blackjack in his 30s, where he quietly lost ~$30M before Vegas ever entered the picture
(good wake up call for all the gamblers here, i

The Economics of a Neocloud
Intense market shortage has created highly lucrative economics and incredibly short payback periods for data center investments
https://degentradingdaily.substack.com/p/the-economics-of-a-neocloud-5-jul

How do you get value out of commodities?

If everyone has access to the same open weight models, where does the value come from?

Value creation comes from the effective application of commodities.

Fuel is a commodity. But two companies with access to the same fuel can produce radically different outcomes. One uses it inefficiently. The other uses it to power a fleet that is tightly coordinated around demand, supply, inventory, routing, labor, timing, and real world exception handling.

The difference is not the fuel.

The difference is the operational system that turns fuel into movement, mo

tweet.text:
Came across an interesting report from SVRC Research called "State of Robotics 2026", published in April. 

Which listed:

1. Figure AI 
2. Agility Robotics $CCXI
3. Apptronik 
4. $TSLA 
5. Boston Dynamics
6. Physical Intelligence 
7. 1X Technologies
8. $AMZN Robotics
9. Covariant
10. Skild AI 

As the National Champions of the United States robotics program. 

"The United States leads the world in where robotics is heading: Fundation models, OpenAI-style scaling laws applied to action, autonomous vehicles. 

While losing the race on where robotics is shipping today." 

Then it frames:

1. Rar

Fireworks is kernelmogging $NBIS and $CRWV on the exact same NVIDIA silicon. 746 tokens per second against 480 and 290.

Run the napkin math. One Fireworks GPU pushes 746 t/s while one Nebius GPU pushes 290, so a single card serves roughly 2.6x the users. To match one Fireworks rack, Nebius has to rack up around two and a half times the hardware.

Kernel optimization is the whole difference. A kernel is the low level GPU code that actually runs the model, and rewriting it lets identical silicon push far more tokens per second. Fireworks built its own through FireAttention while most neoclouds

tweet.text:
The GenAI economy has generated $110 billion in sales over the past 12 months. It is growing fast. On an annualized basis, the revenue run rate exceeds $175 billion.

These numbers took us several months to construct, and as far as we know, it’s the first bottom-up, deduplicated measure of consumer and enterprise AI spending across the full stack.

We are releasing this research today in our first The State of the AI Economy report.

https://intelligence.exponentialview.co/

Came across an interesting report from SVRC Research called "State of Robotics 2026", published in April. 

Which listed:

1. Figure AI 
2. Agility Robotics $CCXI
3. Apptronik 
4. $TSLA 
5. Boston Dynamics
6. Physical Intelligence 
7. 1X Technologies
8. $AMZN Robotics
9. Covariant
10. Skild AI 

As the National Champions of the United States robotics program. 

"The United States leads the world in where robotics is heading: Fundation models, OpenAI-style scaling laws applied to action, autonomous vehicles. 

While losing the race on where robotics is shipping today."

Partners from two top US venture firms, Benchmark and Founders Fund, say they are uncomfortable with the current funding frenzy because it reminds them of 2021.

“I’m very uncomfortable.....because it feels like we're getting back to a point where prices have become untethered from reality. It reminds me a lot of 2021,” says Trae Stephens of Founders Fund.

Everett Randle of Benchmark adds: “The similarity I see with 2021 is that back then there was an almost unanimous sense of optimism because investors were making a tremendous amount of money.”

“I think this duality is what defines a bubble

sample_posts.text:
Terry Watanabe on Gambling Away $350 Million Fortune
• Sold his company (inherited from father) for $400M, netting $350M personally
• Compulsive gambler, ~$127M lost in about a year
• Annual wagering escalated from $4.2M (2005) to $81M (2006) to $825M (2007)
• Currently Terry is broke, living on Social Security
• The core irony he acknowledges: at 5% interest, that fortune would passively throw off ~$17.5M/year forever. His own cited "turning point" was online blackjack in his 30s, where he quietly lost ~$30M before Vegas ever entered the picture
(good wake up call for all the gamblers here, i

Fireworks is kernelmogging $NBIS and $CRWV on the exact same NVIDIA silicon. 746 tokens per second against 480 and 290.

Run the napkin math. One Fireworks GPU pushes 746 t/s while one Nebius GPU pushes 290, so a single card serves roughly 2.6x the users. To match one Fireworks rack, Nebius has to rack up around two and a half times the hardware.

Kernel optimization is the whole difference. A kernel is the low level GPU code that actually runs the model, and rewriting it lets identical silicon push far more tokens per second. Fireworks built its own through FireAttention while most neoclouds
